channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Justice Department prohibits employees from sharing anything related to their work on social media
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has ordered U.S. Justice Department employees not to post anything on social media related to their government work, after a wave of new political appointees took to cheering Trump and castigating his opponents online. The directive, which was emailed to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg Considered Spinning Out Instagram Due to Antitrust Worries
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown on the second day of an antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hertz says hackers stole customer data in vendor breach
item.description: Hertz is notifying customers that hackers may have stolen personal information including credit card details and Social Security numbers during a data breach on one of its vendors.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Notion Mail lets you build the inbox you actually want
item.description: Most email apps have strong opinions about what your inbox should look like. Notion Mail is the opposite. The new offshoot from productivity app Notion is all about flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia reveals it will make AI supercomputers in the U.S. for the first time
item.description: Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI GPT-4.1 models promise improved coding and instruction following
item.description: OpenAI has announced a new family of models, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and GPT-4.1 nano, which it says outperforms GPT-4o and GPT-4o mini “across the board.” In conjunction with the launch of the GPT-4.1 family, OpenAI also announced that it is deprecating GPT-4.5 Preview in the API.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A strong solar storm heads to Earth. Here's what to know about northern lights
item.description: A strong solar storm is headed to Earth and this could cause colorful aurora displays in parts of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption
item.description: The term “synthetic data” refers to artificially-generated data that mimics the patterns and properties of real-world datasets. The post AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Legacy broadcasters account for just more than 10 percent of TV viewership in March
item.description: Legacy media companies amassed only 10 percent of total television viewership during the month of March, according to new figures from Nielsen Media Research. Nielsen's monthly TV viewership report noted Disney, Paramount, Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What ‘Ex Machina’ got right (and wrong) about AI, 10 years later
item.description: Before media outlets began comparing OpenAI’s Sam Altman with the father of the atomic bomb, and before Amazon’s Jeff Bezos got jacked, we had Nathan Bateman, the iron-pumping, AI-developing tech broligarch played by Oscar Isaac in the 2015 film Ex Machina.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Belgian teens arrested with 5,000 smuggled ants as Kenya warns of changing trafficking trends
item.description: Two Belgian teenagers have been charged in Kenya with wildlife piracy after they were found with thousands of ants packed in test tubes in what Kenyan authorities said was part of a trend in trafficking smaller and lesser known species.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why 81% of organizations plan to adopt zero trust by 2026
item.description: VPN technologies have long been the backbone of remote access, but according to new ThreatLabz research, the security risks and performance challenges of VPNs may be rapidly changing the status quo for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A major Google Cloud outage was caused by uninterruptible power supplies being...interrupted
item.description: Google had a “critical battery failure” that took its Columbus, Ohio zone offline for up to six hours on March 29.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia launches improvements at big San Jose office building it leased
item.description: Tech titan Nvidia has begun wide-ranging construction work to prepare a big San Jose office building for its occupancy.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: 5 under-the-radar Kindle tricks to elevate your e-reading
item.description: At first glance, your Kindle might seem like a no-frills reading device: straightforward, minimal, and focused on the basics.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: If you've got a phone running Android 12, it's time to upgrade – here's why
item.description: Security updates for Android 12 and Android 12L have now ceased, which makes the software more vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk’s X faces EU probe over GDPR violations in AI training
item.description: Elon Musk’s X is facing a regulatory probe in Europe over its alleged use of public posts from EU users to train its Grok AI chatbot – an investigation that could set a precedent for how companies use publicly available data under the bloc’s privacy laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Measuring success in dataops, data governance, and data security
item.description: Back in 2006, British mathematician Clive Humby stated that data was the new oil. Like oil, data isn’t useful in its raw state and must be refined, processed, and distributed to deliver value.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intelligence at the edge opens up more risks: how unified SASE can solve it
item.description: In an increasingly mobile and modern workforce, smart technologies such as AI-driven edge solutions and the Internet of Things (IoT) can help enterprises improve productivity and efficiency—whether to address operational roadblocks or respond faster to market demands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why you should update your old dating app profile photos ASAP
item.description: Daters: It might be time to spring clean your dating app profile. More than 50% of young Americans have gone on a date with someone who looked different from their profile photos, according to a new survey from dating app Hily.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cutting-edge drone tech maps land and water with laser accuracy
item.description: New drone technology maps land and water with stunning accuracy, giving researchers and conservationists a new way to understand our planet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network
